h2h_summary: The Netherlands are unbeaten in three all-time meetings, winning once (4-0 in 1978) and drawing twice, with Tunisia yet to defeat the Dutch.
wc_history_home: Tunisia have appeared in seven World Cups (1978–2026), famously becoming the first African team to win a World Cup match with a 3-1 defeat of Mexico in 1978, but have never advanced past the group stage.
wc_history_away: The Netherlands have been World Cup runners-up three times (1974, 1978, 2010) and are one of the most storied nations in tournament history, despite never lifting the trophy.
group_context: Group F consists of Netherlands, Japan, Sweden, and Tunisia, with the top two teams advancing to the round of 32; Netherlands are heavy favourites to top the group, making the second qualifying spot a fierce three-way contest that gives Tunisia's final match against the Dutch enormous significance.
strengths_home: Elite defensive discipline — qualified for 2026 without conceding a single goal across 10 matches
High-energy pressing and structured low-block that frustrates opponents
Strong team spirit and tournament experience with seven World Cup appearances
strengths_away: World-class defensive core led by Virgil van Dijk, one of the best defenders in the world
Prolific attack with Memphis Depay (all-time Dutch top scorer) and Cody Gakpo providing cutting edge
Deep squad with Premier League and Champions League experience across multiple positions
team_style_home: Tunisia deploy a compact 4-2-3-1 built on defensive solidity and disciplined low/mid-block pressing, using direct vertical balls and aerial duels to transition quickly into attack on the counter.
team_style_away: The Netherlands under Koeman play a possession-based 4-2-3-1 that morphs into a fluid 4-3-3 in attack, combining aggressive man-oriented pressing with quick vertical transitions and overlapping full-backs.
recent_news: Netherlands is favored with odds of -750 for the match, while a draw is at +800. The site predicts a 2-0 win for the Netherlands over Tunisia.
context: Group F consists of Netherlands, Japan, Sweden, and Tunisia, with the top two teams advancing to the round of 32; Netherlands are heavy favourites to top the group, making the second qualifying spot a fierce three-way contest that gives Tunisia's final match against the Dutch enormous significance.
